title: Card Dispute Payout Resolution
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: card dispute payout resolution.
assumptions: Rails, accounts, templates, and liquidity buckets are represented as discrete reusable resources with explicit availability predicates.
Manual approvals and compliance checks are modelled as separate boolean/tokenized artifacts consumed during progression.
State transitions are atomic at the model level; concurrent modifications are not represented (implicit serialization).
Templates and routing profiles encode necessary execution parameters; binding a template is sufficient to parameterize execution.
Initial availability predicates in the model (R20, R21, R22, etc.) represent current system capacity and may change only via the specified actions.
risks: Double allocation risk: if resource availability is not synchronized, two parallel flows could reserve the same rail or liquidity bucket.
Stale data risk: decisions based on outdated routing profiles or compliance records could cause misrouted or non-compliant payouts.
Liquidity shortfall: reserved liquidity may not cover final settlement if off-model drains occur outside this workflow.
Timing/race conditions: finalization actions (A30/A31/A32) triggered prematurely could commit payouts before all downstream systems are ready.
Template mismatch: incorrect or stale payout_instruction_template binding could produce invalid instructions for a target rail.
